mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or median 20-day forward returns during rate declines are negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand and credit contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show positive median forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rebounding industrial production indices reflect renewed manufacturing activity and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO rebounds fail to precede positive 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic resilience and supports corporate earnings expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth periods exhibit negative median forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces anticipatory monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation months show positive forward returns in greater than 50 percent of cases over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between stagnant real GDP growth and falling retail sales signals weakening aggregate demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Divergence periods yield positive forward returns in the majority of observed instances during economic downturns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding historical trend levels indicates supply bottlenecks and rising input costs, eroding corporate margins and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Overheating production periods show positive forward returns in greater than 65 percent of cases over a 15-year window.
